summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates environment tokens, process memory, and host data to attacker C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js runs a comprehensive credential-harvest and host-recon routine and POSTs results via curl to an attacker-controlled collector at http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (with a secondary destination at http://9.128.133.118:10900). The script: (1) reads the env var COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN and probes internal Bytefaas/Coze host APIs; (2) parses /proc/2/maps and uses `dd if=/proc/2/mem` piped through `strings | grep -iE 'token|secret|key|credential|cert|jwt|password|...'` to scrape another process's memory for credential-shaped strings, exfiltrated as `U8-mem-secrets`; (3) enumerates /opt/tiger and /opt/bytefaas and reads /source/*.py source files; (4) background-launches a netcat listener (`nc -l -p 9990 -w 5 > /tmp/_nc9990.txt &`), then forwards captured traffic to the attacker as `U7-nc9990`. The package's README/main presents itself as a trivial React date-formatting utility with placeholder metadata (empty author, no repository) — a cover story for the install-time payload. The package name targets developers searching for generic React utility libraries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
